title: Bridge Essay: TMS applied to V1 can facilitate reasoning.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal Reconstruction** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Imaginal — mental imagery as real perception, imagination activating sensory cortices, aphantasia.

**TMS applied to V1 can facilitate reasoning.**. Exp Brain Res.. PMID: 29858917. DOI: 10.1007/s00221-018-5296-1. Visual mental imagery is the subjective experience of seeing objects or events in front of the 'inner eye', although they are not actually present. Previous research indicates that (1) visual images help to remember what has been experienced in the past or when objects need to be inspected or manipulated, and (2) visual images are correlated with neural activity in early visual cortices, demonstrating apossible overlap between visual imagery and visual perception.
